  AND (has([${group_id:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${group_id:singlequote}], group_id)) AND (has([${model:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${model:singlequote}], model)) AND ('$provider' = 'all' OR ('$provider' = 'claude' AND match(model, '^claude-')) OR ('$provider' = 'openai' AND match(model, '^(gpt-|chatgpt-|o[0-9]|text-embedding-|dall-e-|whisper|tts-)')) OR ('$provider' = 'other' AND NOT match(model, '^(claude-|gpt-|chatgpt-|o[0-9]|text-embedding-|dall-e-|whisper|tts-)'))) AND (has([${session_id:singlequote}], '') OR has([${session_id:singlequote}], agent_usage.session_id))
  AND (has([${git_branch:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${git_branch:singlequote}], coalesce(gb.git_branch, ''))) AND (has([${git_repo:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${git_repo:singlequote}], coalesce(gb.git_repo, ''))) AND (has([${agent_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${agent_name:singlequote}], agent_name)) AND (has([${skill_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${skill_name:singlequote}], skill_name)) AND (has([${command_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${command_name:singlequote}], command_name)) AND (has([${mcp_tool:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${mcp_tool:singlequote}], mcp_tool_name))) / 1000000, 2) AS "Tokens out, mil",
  toString(round((SELECT sum(cost) FROM agent_usage LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT session_id, argMax(git_branch, captured_at) AS git_branch, argMax(git_repo, captured_at) AS git_repo
  FROM session_git_branch GROUP BY session_id
) gb ON gb.session_id = agent_usage.session_id WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp) AND (has([${user_id:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${user_id:singlequote}], user_id))
  AND (has([${group_id:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${group_id:singlequote}], group_id)) AND (has([${model:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${model:singlequote}], model)) AND ('$provider' = 'all' OR ('$provider' = 'claude' AND match(model, '^claude-')) OR ('$provider' = 'openai' AND match(model, '^(gpt-|chatgpt-|o[0-9]|text-embedding-|dall-e-|whisper|tts-)')) OR ('$provider' = 'other' AND NOT match(model, '^(claude-|gpt-|chatgpt-|o[0-9]|text-embedding-|dall-e-|whisper|tts-)'))) AND (has([${session_id:singlequote}], '') OR has([${session_id:singlequote}], agent_usage.session_id))
  AND (has([${git_branch:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${git_branch:singlequote}], coalesce(gb.git_branch, ''))) AND (has([${git_repo:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${git_repo:singlequote}], coalesce(gb.git_repo, ''))) AND (has([${agent_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${agent_name:singlequote}], agent_name)) AND (has([${skill_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${skill_name:singlequote}], skill_name)) AND (has([${command_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${command_name:singlequote}], command_name)) AND (has([${mcp_tool:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${mcp_tool:singlequote}], mcp_tool_name))), 2)) AS "Cost, $",
  toString((SELECT count(DISTINCT session_id) FROM agent_events LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT session_id, argMax(git_branch, captured_at) AS git_branch, argMax(git_repo, captured_at) AS git_repo
  FROM session_git_branch GROUP BY session_id
) gb ON gb.session_id = agent_events.session_id WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp) AND (has([${user_id:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${user_id:singlequote}], user_id))
  AND (has([${group_id:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${group_id:singlequote}], group_id)) AND (has([${session_id:singlequote}], '') OR has([${session_id:singlequote}], agent_events.session_id))
  AND (has([${git_branch:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${git_branch:singlequote}], coalesce(gb.git_branch, ''))) AND (has([${git_repo:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${git_repo:singlequote}], coalesce(gb.git_repo, '')))
  AND ((has([${model:singlequote}], '__all__') AND '$provider' = 'all') OR agent_events.session_id IN (
    SELECT session_id FROM agent_usage
    WHERE (has([${model:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${model:singlequote}], model))
      AND ('$provider' = 'all' OR ('$provider' = 'claude' AND match(model, '^claude-')) OR ('$provider' = 'openai' AND match(model, '^(gpt-|chatgpt-|o[0-9]|text-embedding-|dall-e-|whisper|tts-)')) OR ('$provider' = 'other' AND NOT match(model, '^(claude-|gpt-|chatgpt-|o[0-9]|text-embedding-|dall-e-|whisper|tts-)')))
  )) AND (has([${agent_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${agent_name:singlequote}], agent_name)) AND (has([${skill_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${skill_name:singlequote}], skill_name)) AND (has([${command_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${command_name:singlequote}], command_name)) AND (NOT startsWith(tool_name, 'mcp__') OR (has([${mcp_tool:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${mcp_tool:singlequote}], tool_name))))) AS "Sessions",
  toString((SELECT count(DISTINCT user_id) FROM agent_events LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT session_id, argMax(git_branch, captured_at) AS git_branch, argMax(git_repo, captured_at) AS git_repo
  FROM session_git_branch GROUP BY session_id
) gb ON gb.session_id = agent_events.session_id WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp) AND (has([${user_id:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${user_id:singlequote}], user_id))
  AND (has([${group_id:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${group_id:singlequote}], group_id)) AND (has([${session_id:singlequote}], '') OR has([${session_id:singlequote}], agent_events.session_id))
  AND (has([${git_branch:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${git_branch:singlequote}], coalesce(gb.git_branch, ''))) AND (has([${git_repo:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${git_repo:singlequote}], coalesce(gb.git_repo, '')))
  AND ((has([${model:singlequote}], '__all__') AND '$provider' = 'all') OR agent_events.session_id IN (
    SELECT session_id FROM agent_usage
    WHERE (has([${model:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${model:singlequote}], model))
      AND ('$provider' = 'all' OR ('$provider' = 'claude' AND match(model, '^claude-')) OR ('$provider' = 'openai' AND match(model, '^(gpt-|chatgpt-|o[0-9]|text-embedding-|dall-e-|whisper|tts-)')) OR ('$provider' = 'other' AND NOT match(model, '^(claude-|gpt-|chatgpt-|o[0-9]|text-embedding-|dall-e-|whisper|tts-)')))
  )) AND (has([${agent_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${agent_name:singlequote}], agent_name)) AND (has([${skill_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${skill_name:singlequote}], skill_name)) AND (has([${command_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${command_name:singlequote}], command_name)) AND (NOT startsWith(tool_name, 'mcp__') OR (has([${mcp_tool:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${mcp_tool:singlequote}], tool_name))))) AS "Users"

spec.title: ExitPlanMode: user prompt -> proposed plan
spec.description: Every ExitPlanMode tool call paired with the user prompt from the same turn (agent_events joined to agent_messages on session_id+timestamp) and the plan text reported separately by hooks/report_plan_proposal.py (a PreToolUse hook - LiteLLM's raw_payload doesn't carry ExitPlanMode's arguments, they come back empty), matched via an ASOF JOIN on session_id + nearest captured_at at or after the triggering event's timestamp. Click the magnifier on prompt_text/plan_text to see full text.

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  ev.timestamp,
  ev.user_id,
  usr.user_name AS user_name,
  ev.session_id,
  ev.turn_id,
  ev.agent_name,
  ev.skill_name,
  ev.status,
  m.prompt_text,
  pp.plan_text
FROM agent_events ev
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT user_id, argMax(user_name, updated_at) AS user_name
  FROM ai_gateway_users
  GROUP BY user_id
) usr ON usr.user_id = ev.user_id
ASOF LEFT JOIN plan_proposals pp
  ON pp.session_id = ev.session_id AND pp.captured_at >= ev.timestamp
LEFT JOIN agent_messages m
  ON m.session_id = ev.session_id AND m.timestamp = ev.timestamp
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT session_id, argMax(git_branch, captured_at) AS git_branch, argMax(git_repo, captured_at) AS git_repo
  FROM session_git_branch GROUP BY session_id
) gb ON gb.session_id = ev.session_id
WHERE ev.tool_name = 'ExitPlanMode'
  AND $__timeFilter(ev.timestamp)
  AND (has([${session_id:singlequote}], '') OR has([${session_id:singlequote}], ev.session_id))
  AND (has([${git_branch:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${git_branch:singlequote}], coalesce(gb.git_branch, ''))) AND (has([${git_repo:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${git_repo:singlequote}], coalesce(gb.git_repo, '')))
  AND ((has([${model:singlequote}], '__all__') AND '$provider' = 'all') OR ev.session_id IN (
    SELECT session_id FROM agent_usage
    WHERE (has([${model:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${model:singlequote}], model))
      AND ('$provider' = 'all' OR ('$provider' = 'claude' AND match(model, '^claude-')) OR ('$provider' = 'openai' AND match(model, '^(gpt-|chatgpt-|o[0-9]|text-embedding-|dall-e-|whisper|tts-)')) OR ('$provider' = 'other' AND NOT match(model, '^(claude-|gpt-|chatgpt-|o[0-9]|text-embedding-|dall-e-|whisper|tts-)')))
  ))
  AND (has([${user_id:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${user_id:singlequote}], ev.user_id))
  AND (has([${group_id:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${group_id:singlequote}], ev.group_id))
  AND (has([${agent_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${agent_name:singlequote}], ev.agent_name))
  AND (has([${skill_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${skill_name:singlequote}], ev.skill_name))
  AND (has([${command_name:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${command_name:singlequote}], ev.command_name))
ORDER BY ev.timestamp DESC

spec.title: Trace: $session_id
spec.description: One call tree per session, monospace. Right after the header comes a session summary block (duration, cost, tokens in/out, models, prompt count, tool-call count, agents/skills used - two values per line). User prompts/comments and model replies are shown in full (no truncation) and wrap across lines in the viewer; only tool-call argument previews stay short and column-aligned with a right-hand stats block, since a reply/prompt of unbounded length can't share that fixed-width column without being cut back down. Everything before the session's first genuine (non-harness) user message is dropped - a silent auto-retry or warm-up call that never showed up in the CLI itself would otherwise appear as a confusing orphan row. Timestamps only on important nodes - user prompts/comments (marked ● when real, ○ when it's harness-injected noise: goal-check judge calls, suggestion-mode prompts, background stop-hook notifications, conversation-title-generation calls - best-effort detection, not exhaustive, since agent_messages.prompt_text captures any 'user'-role turn verbatim, harness boilerplate included - see _last_user_text's docstring in clickhouse_ingest.py), agent spawns, and replies - not every plain tool call. A leading <system-reminder>...</system-reminder> block is stripped before display, and a <command-args> block is shown as '/command <args>' when present. Tool calls prefer showing their file_path argument when one exists. A row whose own LLM call failed (status='failure') shows the error type from a join to event_sources.raw_payload_full's error_information (e.g. rate_limit_error) instead of blank - raw_payload was dropped from agent_events itself, event_sources is the only place the original payload still lives, keyed on litellm_call_id - distinct from a row with failed_tool_name/failed_tool_error set, which means THIS call succeeded but is reacting to a tool that failed one step earlier (shown as an indented 🚨 note above the row) - the two are not the same thing, see the failed_tool_* column comments in schema.sql. Latency/tokens/cost are dimmed (opacity) and tokens are abbreviated (12.3k tok); latency shows in seconds once past 100ms. Shows nothing when no session is selected, one tree for a single session, one tree per session when "All" is selected (bounded by the session_id variable's own 200-session cap). Agent-spawn attribution (which agent_invocations row belongs to which Agent tool_use call) is a best-effort ASOF match on nearest spawned_at, not a real parent link. Nesting is a single level - the data model has no parent link for a sub-sub-agent.

spec.rawSql:
WITH selected AS ( SELECT DISTINCT session_id FROM agent_events WHERE (has([${session_id:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${session_id:singlequote}], session_id)) ), scoped_events AS ( SELECT ev.session_id AS session_id, ev.timestamp AS timestamp, ev.tool_name AS tool_name, ev.litellm_call_id AS litellm_call_id, ev.agent_invocation_id AS agent_invocation_id, ev.command_name AS command_name, ev.status AS status, ev.latency_ms AS latency_ms, ev.calculated_type AS calculated_type, ev.calculated_payload AS calculated_payload, ev.failed_tool_name AS failed_tool_name, ev.failed_tool_error AS failed_tool_error, m.prompt_text AS prompt_text, m.response_text AS response_text, lagInFrame(m.prompt_text) OVER ( PARTITION BY ev.session_id, ev.agent_invocation_id ORDER BY ev.timestamp ) AS prev_prompt_text, -- A newly-spawned subagent's own conversation begins with a "user"-role -- message that is actually the task/prompt the orchestrator's model -- handed it via the Agent tool_use's own arguments - not anything a -- human typed. Every subsequent turn inside that subagent still resolves -- to this same text as its "last human-originated" turn (no further -- genuine human input ever arrives mid-subagent), so it only survives -- prompt_calc's dedup once, on the very first row of the invocation. -- row_number()=1 within (session_id, agent_invocation_id) identifies -- that first row, used below (is_agent_task) to render it with the -- assistant-text marker/style instead of the real-user-prompt ❯ marker. row_number() OVER ( PARTITION BY ev.session_id, ev.agent_invocation_id ORDER BY ev.timestamp ) AS rn_in_invocation FROM agent_events ev INNER JOIN selected s ON s.session_id = ev.session_id -- litellm_call_id (unique per call, confirmed) replaces the old -- m.timestamp = ev.timestamp equality join, which could silently pair a -- row with the wrong agent_messages row whenever two calls landed in the -- same millisecond (parallel subagent dispatch, MCP call bursts) - both -- tables now carry litellm_call_id in their ORDER BY for exactly this -- reason. Guarded by ev.litellm_call_id != '' so a row ingested before -- this column existed (permanently blank - see schema.sql's agent_events -- comment) simply fails to join rather than colliding with every other -- blank-call-id row in the same session. LEFT JOIN agent_messages m ON m.session_id = ev.session_id AND ev.litellm_call_id != '' AND m.litellm_call_id = ev.litellm_call_id ), usage_by_call AS ( SELECT u.session_id AS session_id, u.litellm_call_id AS litellm_call_id, sum((u.input_tokens - (1 - $include_cache_tokens) * (u.cache_creation_tokens + u.cache_read_tokens)) + u.output_tokens) AS tok, sum(u.cost) AS cost FROM agent_usage u INNER JOIN selected s ON s.session_id = u.session_id WHERE u.litellm_call_id != '' GROUP BY u.session_id, u.litellm_call_id ), -- A background subagent runs concurrently with the orchestrator's own -- continued work, so sorting every row purely by its own real timestamp -- interleaves two unrelated threads of activity (the subagent's steps and -- whatever the orchestrator did *while waiting*) into one jumbled stream. -- child_anchor gives every agent_invocation_id a single "anchor" timestamp -- - the orchestrator's own nearest-preceding Agent-spawn row - so the -- whole subagent block sorts as one contiguous unit right after its spawn -- point, instead of scattering across the orchestrator's concurrent rows. -- This is a genuine ASOF heuristic (no real parent link exists - see -- agent_invocations' own comment in schema.sql), not one of the equality -- joins fixed by litellm_call_id above. agent_spawn_events AS ( SELECT ev.session_id AS session_id, ev.timestamp AS timestamp FROM agent_events ev INNER JOIN selected s ON s.session_id = ev.session_id WHERE ev.calculated_type = 'agent_spawn' AND ev.agent_invocation_id = '' ), child_anchor_raw AS ( SELECT ai.session_id AS session_id, ai.agent_id AS agent_id, se.timestamp AS anchor_ts FROM agent_invocations ai INNER JOIN selected s ON s.session_id = ai.session_id ASOF LEFT JOIN agent_spawn_events se ON se.session_id = ai.session_id AND ai.spawned_at >= se.timestamp ), child_anchor AS ( -- agent_invocations can carry more than one row per agent_id (no FINAL -- applied to its ReplacingMergeTree here) - dedup to exactly one row per -- (session_id, agent_id) or the later join in the final SELECT would -- silently multiply every one of that subagent's event rows. SELECT session_id, agent_id, if(any(anchor_ts) = toDateTime64('1970-01-01 00:00:00', 3), NULL, any(anchor_ts)) AS anchor_ts FROM child_anchor_raw GROUP BY session_id, agent_id ), session_header AS ( SELECT s.session_id AS session_id, any(ev.user_id) AS user_id, argMax(gb.git_branch, gb.captured_at) AS git_branch, argMax(gb.git_repo, gb.captured_at) AS git_repo, min(ev.timestamp) AS first_ts FROM selected s INNER JOIN agent_events ev ON ev.session_id = s.session_id LEFT JOIN session_git_branch gb ON gb.session_id = s.session_id GROUP BY s.session_id ), stats_tokens AS ( SELECT u.session_id AS session_id, sum((u.input_tokens - (1 - $include_cache_tokens) * (u.cache_creation_tokens + u.cache_read_tokens))) AS tok_in, sum(u.output_tokens) AS tok_out, sum(u.cost) AS cost_total, arrayStringConcat(groupUniqArray(u.model), ', ') AS models FROM agent_usage u INNER JOIN selected s ON s.session_id = u.session_id WHERE u.model != '' GROUP BY u.session_id ), -- agent_events.agent_name is blank whenever the subagent's own rows raced -- ahead of the orchestrator's Agent tool_use/tool_result (best-effort lookup, -- see _agent_name_and_version_for_invocation's docstring in -- clickhouse_ingest.py) - agent_invocations.subagent_type is the reliable -- source of "which subagents actually ran", so the two are unioned rather -- than trusting agent_events alone. agent_names_raw AS ( SELECT ev.session_id AS session_id, ev.agent_name AS name FROM agent_events ev INNER JOIN selected s ON s.session_id = ev.session_id WHERE ev.agent_name != '' UNION ALL SELECT ai.session_id AS session_id, splitByChar('_', ai.subagent_type)[1] AS name FROM agent_invocations ai INNER JOIN selected s ON s.session_id = ai.session_id WHERE ai.subagent_type != '' AND splitByChar('_', ai.subagent_type)[1] != '' ), stats_agent_names AS ( -- arrayFilter is defense-in-depth against any stray '' sneaking into the -- array (e.g. a future source added to the UNION above without its own -- empty-string guard) - arrayStringConcat would otherwise render it as a -- trailing ", " with nothing after it. SELECT session_id AS session_id, arrayStringConcat(arrayFilter(x -> x != '', groupUniqArray(name)), ', ') AS agents FROM agent_names_raw GROUP BY session_id ), stats_skills AS ( SELECT ev.session_id AS session_id, arrayStringConcat(groupUniqArrayIf(ev.skill_name, ev.skill_name != ''), ', ') AS skills FROM agent_events ev INNER JOIN selected s ON s.session_id = ev.session_id GROUP BY ev.session_id ), stats_time AS ( SELECT ev.session_id AS session_id, dateDiff('second', min(ev.timestamp), max(ev.timestamp)) AS dur_sec, countIf(ev.tool_name != '') AS n_tools FROM agent_events ev INNER JOIN selected s ON s.session_id = ev.session_id GROUP BY ev.session_id ), -- prompt_calc/prompt_display/prompt_final are computed here (moved ahead of -- session_stats) so that stats_prompts below can reuse the same is_real -- classification used for the tie=2 marker glyph (❯ vs ○/●/🧐), instead of -- a separate, cruder "any row with prompt_text" count that lumps real user -- requests together with sys-checks/title-gen/suggestion-mode/judge calls. -- is_sys/is_sugg/is_judge/is_transcript_handoff/is_title/is_interrupt/ -- is_webpage all used to be recomputed here via a startsWith(cleaned0, ...) -- prefix chain duplicating clickhouse_ingest.py's _classify_event - now -- they're a direct equality read of that same classifier's own output -- (agent_events.calculated_type), computed once at ingest instead of twice. prompt_calc AS ( SELECT sr.session_id, sr.timestamp AS ts, sr.litellm_call_id AS litellm_call_id, trimBoth(replaceRegexpOne(sr.prompt_text, '(?s)^.*?\s*', '')) AS cleaned0, -- Not every slash command populates ... -- with its real typed text - '/goal' does, but '/plan' (and possibly -- other commands) leaves that tag empty and instead puts the actual -- user-typed text as plain text right after the -- '...' block. Fall back to -- that when the tag itself is empty, so ANY slash command displays -- cleanly, not just '/goal' specifically. extract(sr.prompt_text, '(?s)(.*?)') AS cmd_args_tag, sr.prompt_text LIKE '%%' AS has_stdout, trimBoth(replaceRegexpOne(sr.prompt_text, '(?s)^.*', '')) AS after_stdout, sr.command_name AS command_name, sr.agent_invocation_id AS agent_invocation_id, sr.calculated_type AS calculated_type, (sr.agent_invocation_id != '' AND sr.rn_in_invocation = 1) AS is_agent_task FROM scoped_events sr WHERE sr.prompt_text != '' AND (sr.prev_prompt_text IS NULL OR sr.prev_prompt_text != sr.prompt_text) ), prompt_display AS ( SELECT session_id, ts, litellm_call_id, agent_invocation_id, is_agent_task, calculated_type = 'system_notification' AS is_sys, calculated_type = 'suggestion_mode' AS is_sugg, -- Real judge calls (the /goal stopping-condition check) are classified -- as 'judge_call' by _classify_event (same "Based on the conversation -- transcript above" prefix check, now run once at ingest). Messages -- classified 'transcript_handoff' (prefixed '') are a -- different mechanism (a background harm/severity classifier call, not -- a context-handoff/compaction-resume mechanism) - see is_transcript_handoff. calculated_type = 'judge_call' AS is_judge, calculated_type = 'transcript_handoff' AS is_transcript_handoff, calculated_type = 'title_gen' AS is_title, calculated_type = 'interrupted' AS is_interrupt, calculated_type = 'webpage_content' AS is_webpage, if(cmd_args_tag != '', cmd_args_tag, if(command_name != '' AND has_stdout, after_stdout, cmd_args_tag) ) AS cmd_args, (if(cmd_args_tag != '', cmd_args_tag, if(command_name != '' AND has_stdout, after_stdout, cmd_args_tag) ) != '') AS is_cmd, command_name, cleaned0, if(is_sys, trimBoth(substringUTF8(cleaned0, positionUTF8(cleaned0, '\n\n') + 2, 2000)), '') AS sys_tail FROM prompt_calc ), prompt_final AS ( SELECT session_id, ts, litellm_call_id, agent_invocation_id, is_webpage, is_sugg, is_judge, is_title, is_agent_task, (is_cmd OR NOT (is_sys OR is_sugg OR is_judge OR is_title OR is_transcript_handoff)) AS is_real, multiIf( is_cmd, concat('/', command_name, ' ', substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(cmd_args, '[ \t]+', ' '), '\n{2,}', '\n'), 1, 1500)), is_interrupt, concat('[interrupted] ', substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(trimBoth(replaceRegexpOne(cleaned0, '^\[Request interrupted by user\]\s*', '')), '[ \t]+', ' '), '\n{2,}', '\n'), 1, 1500)), is_sugg, substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(cleaned0, '[ \t]+', ' '), '\n{2,}', '\n'), 1, 1500), is_judge, '[goal-check judge call]', is_title, '[title-gen call]', is_transcript_handoff, '[background] severity check', is_sys, if(extract(sys_tail, '(?s)(.*?)') != '', concat('[background] ', extract(sys_tail, '(.*?)'), ': ', substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(extract(sys_tail, '(?s)(.*?)'), '[ \t]+', ' '), '\n{2,}', '\n'), 1, 1500)), if(sys_tail != '', concat('[background] ', substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(sys_tail, '[ \t]+', ' '), '\n{2,}', '\n'), 1, 1500)), '[background check]') ), -- WebFetch page dumps ("Web page content: ---") can be enormous - -- always hard-cut these to a short preview regardless of the general -- 1500-char cap below. This is really the tool's own fetched content -- being echoed back, not something the user typed - shown with the -- reply marker (●), not a ❯/○ prompt marker, see the tie=2 line below. is_webpage, concat(substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(cleaned0, '[ \t]+', ' '), '\n{2,}', '\n'), 1, 100), '...'), substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(cleaned0, '[ \t]+', ' '), '\n{2,}', '\n'), 1, 1500) ) AS display FROM prompt_display ), stats_prompts AS ( -- Only count genuine user-authored requests (the same is_real flag that -- drives the ❯ marker glyph below), excluding is_webpage (which overrides -- is_real's marker to ● even though is_real can be true for it) - anything -- else (sys-checks, title-gen, suggestion-mode, judge calls, transcript -- handoffs) is harness noise, not something the user asked for, and must -- not be lumped into "Prompts:". is_agent_task (a subagent's own spawn -- task, echoed back as its first "user"-role turn - see is_agent_task's -- own comment above) is likewise not user-authored and excluded here too. SELECT session_id AS session_id, countIf(is_real AND NOT is_webpage AND NOT is_agent_task) AS n_prompts FROM prompt_final GROUP BY session_id ), session_stats AS ( SELECT st.session_id AS session_id, concat( 'Duration: ', if(st.dur_sec >= 3600, concat(toString(intDiv(st.dur_sec, 3600)), 'h ', toString(intDiv(st.dur_sec % 3600, 60)), 'm'), if(st.dur_sec >= 60, concat(toString(intDiv(st.dur_sec, 60)), 'm ', toString(st.dur_sec % 60), 's'), concat(toString(st.dur_sec), 's'))), '\n', 'Cost: $', toString(round(coalesce(sk.cost_total, 0), 4)), '\n', 'Tokens: ', toString(round(coalesce(sk.tok_in, 0) / 1000., 1)), 'k in / ', toString(round(coalesce(sk.tok_out, 0) / 1000., 1)), 'k out\n', 'Model(s): ', coalesce(sk.models, ''), '\n', 'Prompts: ', toString(coalesce(pr.n_prompts, 0)), '\n', 'Tool calls: ', toString(st.n_tools), '\n', 'Agents: ', if(coalesce(an.agents, '') = '', 'none', an.agents), '\n', 'Skills: ', if(coalesce(sl.skills, '') = '', 'none', sl.skills), '\n', 'Git: ', if(coalesce(sh.git_repo, '') = '' AND coalesce(sh.git_branch, '') = '', 'unknown', concat(if(sh.git_repo != '', sh.git_repo, '?'), ':', if(sh.git_branch != '', sh.git_branch, '?'))) ) AS block FROM stats_time st LEFT JOIN stats_tokens sk ON sk.session_id = st.session_id LEFT JOIN stats_agent_names an ON an.session_id = st.session_id LEFT JOIN stats_skills sl ON sl.session_id = st.session_id LEFT JOIN stats_prompts pr ON pr.session_id = st.session_id LEFT JOIN session_header sh ON sh.session_id = st.session_id ), tool_render AS ( -- Precomputed once per tool-call/skill-call row so the escaped args string -- can be reused (built once, then re-wrapped in a without -- recomputing the JSON extraction) instead of duplicating the per-field -- preference chain. calculated_payload's 'tools' array (tool_call) and -- 'args' object (skill_call) already hold the decoded argument structure -- computed once at ingest by _classify_event - JSONExtractRaw pulls each -- one's 'args' object back out as raw JSON text, the same shape the old -- raw_payload-based JSONExtractString(..., 'arguments') used to hand -- back, so every per-field JSONExtractString(args_json, 'file_path'/ -- 'command'/...) below is unchanged. Prefer file_path (Read/Write/Edit), -- command (Bash), url (WebFetch), query (WebSearch/web_search), or -- task_id (TaskStop) over the raw JSON args blob. AskUserQuestion is -- special-cased to an empty preview: its args are a structured -- questions[] array, already fully rendered as its own nested grey lines -- (tie=4 below) - showing the raw/truncated JSON here too would be -- redundant and read as broken. full_args_attr mirrors escaped_args but -- WITHOUT the per-branch substringUTF8 truncation, for use in a title="" -- tooltip showing the complete value on hover - it additionally escapes -- '"' to '"' since it sits inside an HTML attribute, not just -- element content. SELECT session_id AS session_id, litellm_call_id AS litellm_call_id, -- '[ \t\n\r]+' -> ' ' collapses literal newlines/carriage-returns too -- (not just spaces/tabs), not merely for cosmetic dedup - the whole -- panel renders inside a 
 (literal whitespace preserved), so any
    -- raw '\n' in a multi-line argument (a multi-line Bash 'command' being
    -- the common case) would otherwise render as an actual visual line
    -- break in the middle of what must stay a single compact
    -- "├─ ToolName args" line. Applied to every branch (including
    -- file_path/url/query/task_id, which previously had no whitespace
    -- collapsing at all) for consistency, even though those are less
    -- likely to contain embedded newlines in practice.
    --
    -- raw_args is the same truncated preview BEFORE HTML-escaping - kept as
    -- its own column (not just an inline expression inside escaped_args)
    -- because column-padding math downstream must measure visual/rendered
    -- width, and escaping inflates character count without inflating
    -- rendered width: '>' -> '>' is 1 visible char but 4 string chars.
    -- Padding against escaped_args's length previously produced too few
    -- trailing spaces (and an inconsistent pad/no-pad threshold decision)
    -- for any arg containing '&'/'<'/'>' (shell redirects '2>', heredocs
    -- '<<', etc.) - see this panel's own doc for the full diagnosis.
    if(tool_name = 'AskUserQuestion', '',
      multiIf(
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'file_path') != '',
          -- Every field branch below now shares one unified 90-char
          -- truncation cap (previously varied per field: 120 for paths,
          -- 100 for the mcp 'sql' arg, 70 for command/query/fallback) - see
          -- this panel's own doc, "Unified 90-char truncation cap" section.
          substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'file_path'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '), 1, 90),
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'command') != '',
          substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'command'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '), 1, 90),
        -- MCP-style tool calls (e.g. mcp__clickhouse__query) carry their
        -- payload under a 'sql' argument rather than 'command'.
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'sql') != '',
          substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'sql'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '), 1, 90),
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'url') != '',
          substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'url'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '), 1, 90),
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'query') != '',
          substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'query'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '), 1, 90),
        -- Monitor tool calls carry a 'description' argument.
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'description') != '',
          substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'description'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '), 1, 90),
        -- SendMessage tool calls carry a 'summary' argument.
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'summary') != '',
          substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'summary'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '), 1, 90),
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'task_id') != '',
          concat('task_id: ', replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'task_id'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' ')),

    ) AS raw_args,
    replaceAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(raw_args, '&', '&'), '<', '<'), '>', '>') AS escaped_args,
    if(tool_name = 'AskUserQuestion', '', replaceAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(
      multiIf(
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'file_path') != '', replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'file_path'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '),
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'command') != '', replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'command'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '),
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'sql') != '', replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'sql'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '),
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'url') != '', replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'url'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '),
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'query') != '', replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'query'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '),
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'description') != '', replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'description'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '),
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'summary') != '', replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'summary'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '),
        JSONExtractString(args_json, 'task_id') != '', concat('task_id: ', replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(args_json, 'task_id'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' ')),

      ), '&', '&'), '<', '<'), '>', '>'), '"', '"'
    )) AS full_args_attr
  FROM (
    SELECT session_id, litellm_call_id, tool_name,
      if(calculated_type = 'skill_call',
        JSONExtractRaw(calculated_payload, 'args'),
        JSONExtractRaw(calculated_payload, 'tools', 1, 'args')
      ) AS args_json
    FROM scoped_events
    WHERE tool_name != '' AND tool_name != 'Agent' AND status != 'failure'
      AND calculated_type IN ('tool_call', 'skill_call')
  )
),
-- raw_payload is gone from agent_events - a failed call's own error type
-- (e.g. rate_limit_error) isn't captured by calculated_type/calculated_payload
-- either (_classify_event never runs a failure-specific branch, since a
-- failed call has no response.choices to classify), so this is one of the
-- rare cases genuinely needing the full original payload back, via a join
-- to event_sources on litellm_call_id (the same key that table is itself
-- keyed/ordered on).
failure_error AS (
  SELECT sr.session_id AS session_id, sr.litellm_call_id AS litellm_call_id,
    JSONExtractString(JSONExtractString(es.raw_payload_full, 'error_information', 'error_message'), 'error', 'type') AS error_type
  FROM scoped_events sr
  INNER JOIN event_sources es ON es.litellm_call_id = sr.litellm_call_id
  WHERE sr.status = 'failure' AND sr.litellm_call_id != ''
),
tie2_ts AS (
  -- Every (session_id, litellm_call_id) that actually renders a tie=2
  -- prompt-marker line (i.e. survives that branch's own is_title/is_sugg
  -- filter) - used so the tie=3 branch (reply, and Agent-spawn/failure
  -- rows) can tell whether a visible timestamp was already shown on the
  -- marker line immediately above it in the tree (same underlying call),
  -- and suppress its own otherwise-duplicate timestamp rather than
  -- stacking two identical HH:MM:SS values on adjacent lines.
  SELECT DISTINCT pf.session_id AS session_id, pf.litellm_call_id AS litellm_call_id
  FROM prompt_final pf
  WHERE NOT pf.is_title AND NOT pf.is_sugg
),
first_real AS (
  -- Anything before the session's first genuine (non-harness) user message is
  -- pre-conversation noise (a silent auto-retry, a warm-up ping) invisible in
  -- the CLI itself - drop it rather than showing a confusing orphan FAIL row.
  SELECT session_id, min(ts) AS ts
  FROM prompt_final
  WHERE is_real
  GROUP BY session_id
)
SELECT session_id,
  arrayStringConcat(
    arrayMap(x -> x.4, arraySort(x -> (x.1, x.2, x.3), groupArray((sort_ts, tie, ts, line)))),
    '\n'
  ) AS tree
FROM (
  SELECT session_id, first_ts AS ts, first_ts AS sort_ts, 0 AS tie,
    concat('') AS line
  FROM session_header

  UNION ALL

  SELECT ss.session_id, sh.first_ts AS ts, sh.first_ts AS sort_ts, 1 AS tie,
    concat('\n', ss.block, '\n\n') AS line
  FROM session_stats ss
  INNER JOIN session_header sh ON sh.session_id = ss.session_id

  UNION ALL

  -- A subagent's own rows (prompt markers here, event lines below) sort by
  -- child_anchor's anchor_ts - the orchestrator's own spawn point - rather
  -- than their own real timestamp, so the whole block stays contiguous
  -- instead of interleaving with whatever the orchestrator did while the
  -- subagent was still running in the background. See child_anchor's own
  -- comment above for why.
  SELECT pf.session_id, pf.ts, if(pf.agent_invocation_id != '', coalesce(ca.anchor_ts, pf.ts), pf.ts) AS sort_ts, 2 AS tie,
    concat(
      -- The visible HH:MM:SS is just link text - the href carries this
      -- call's litellm_call_id (unique per call, confirmed) so the detail
      -- table below can match the exact agent_events row, replacing the
      -- old toString(pf.ts) value: two different rows sharing the same
      -- millisecond (parallel subagent dispatch, MCP bursts) would
      -- otherwise be ambiguous from a timestamp string alone, which
      -- litellm_call_id cannot be. A plain href replaces the *entire*
      -- query string on click (not just adds to it) - onclick+
      -- URLSearchParams.set() would merge instead, but Grafana's own HTML
      -- sanitizer (textUtil.sanitize(), on by default - see
      -- disableSanitizeHtml in grafana.ini) strips inline event handlers
      -- like onclick from Text-panel HTML, so that approach silently does
      -- nothing on click. Explicitly re-stating var-session_id (this row's
      -- own session, not whatever else was selected) alongside
      -- var-trace_ts keeps the session correct; the time range and active
      -- tab aren't preserved this way, but this panel's own query never
      -- filters by time range anyway.
      '', formatDateTime(pf.ts, '%H:%i:%S'), '', '  ',
      -- is_agent_task: a subagent's own spawn task, echoed back as its
      -- first "user"-role turn (see is_agent_task's own comment above) -
      -- authored by the orchestrator's model, not typed by a human. Unlike
      -- every other marker in this branch, is_agent_task ALWAYS renders as
      -- a bare bullet with no indent and no tree connector (└─/├─),
      -- regardless of whether the echoed task text is present or
      -- empty/blank - this row is never nested visually under anything,
      -- so an indent+connector prefix here would misleadingly imply
      -- parentage that doesn't exist for this node.
      if(pf.is_agent_task,
        '● ',
        concat(
          if(pf.agent_invocation_id != '', '   ', ''),
          if(pf.is_webpage, '● ', if(pf.is_judge, '🧐 ', if(pf.is_real, '❯ ', '○ ')))
        )
      ),
      replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(pf.display, '&', '&'), '<', '<'), '>', '>'), '\*\*([^*\n]+?)\*\*', '\1'), '`([^`\n]+?)`', '\1'), '(?m)^(#{1,3}) (.*?)$', '\2'), '\n', '
          '), '(^|\s)(/[a-zA-Z][\w-]*)', '\1\2')
    ) AS line
  FROM prompt_final pf
  INNER JOIN first_real fr ON fr.session_id = pf.session_id
  LEFT JOIN child_anchor ca ON ca.session_id = pf.session_id AND ca.agent_id = pf.agent_invocation_id
  WHERE pf.ts >= fr.ts AND NOT pf.is_title AND NOT pf.is_sugg

  UNION ALL

  SELECT sr.session_id, sr.timestamp AS ts, if(sr.agent_invocation_id != '', coalesce(ca.anchor_ts, sr.timestamp), sr.timestamp) AS sort_ts, 3 AS tie,
    concat(
      if(sr.failed_tool_name != '',
        concat(
          '', formatDateTime(sr.timestamp, '%H:%i:%S'), '', '     🚨 ',
          replaceAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(sr.failed_tool_name, '&', '&'), '<', '<'), '>', '>'),
          ' failed: ',
          replaceAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(
            substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(sr.failed_tool_error, '[ \t]+', ' '), '\n{2,}', '\n'), 1, 200),
            '&', '&'), '<', '<'), '>', '>'),
          ''
        ),
        ''
      ),
      -- Reply rows show the model's full text (can wrap across lines in the
      -- viewer) so they never get squeezed into the fixed-width tool-call
      -- column - only bounded-length tool-call/agent/error lines get padded
      -- into a stats column that lines up visually. If a tie=2 marker line
      -- was already shown immediately above for this exact call (the
      -- reply's own prompt, e.g. an is_sys/background-check or
      -- is_transcript_handoff row), suppress this reply's own timestamp
      -- (invisible link, same rendered width) instead of stacking an
      -- identical HH:MM:SS on both lines.
      if(sr.tool_name = '' AND sr.status != 'failure',
        concat(
          if(t2.litellm_call_id IS NOT NULL,
            concat('', formatDateTime(sr.timestamp, '%H:%i:%S'), ''),
            concat('', formatDateTime(sr.timestamp, '%H:%i:%S'), '')
          ), '  ',
          -- If the model's reply text is itself empty/blank (nothing to
          -- attach the tree connector to), drop the indent AND the └─
          -- connector entirely and render just the bare bullet, instead of
          -- padding out to the stats column with an indent+connector and no
          -- visible text after it (or the old '[empty reply]' placeholder,
          -- now removed - the bare bullet communicates "empty" on its own).
          if(trimBoth(coalesce(sr.response_text, '')) = '',
            concat('● ', '  '),
            concat(
              if(sr.agent_invocation_id != '', '   ', ''),
              if(startsWith(sr.response_text, 'Web page content'), '   └─ ● ', '└─ ● '),
              replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(
                if(startsWith(sr.response_text, 'Web page content'),
                  concat(substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(sr.response_text, '[ \t]+', ' '), '\n{2,}', '\n'), 1, 100), '...'),
                  substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(sr.response_text, '[ \t]+', ' '), '\n{2,}', '\n'), 1, 1500)
                ),
                '&', '&'), '<', '<'), '>', '>'
              ), '\*\*([^*\n]+?)\*\*', '\1'), '`([^`\n]+?)`', '\1'), '(?m)^(#{1,3}) (.*?)$', '\2'), '\n', '
          '),
              '  '
            )
          )
        ),
        if(sr.tool_name != '' AND sr.tool_name != 'Agent' AND sr.status != 'failure',
          -- Real tool call: a short fixed gap (not a wide fixed-width pad)
          -- between the args text and the stats block - a large pad width
          -- left a huge gap after short args (e.g. "Bash cat Makefile")
          -- and, before this, also risked truncating long args and
          -- silently breaking the replaceOne() span-wrap below (see the
          -- padding-vs-truncation gotcha in this agent's own doc).
          -- Column alignment: pad only the "├─ ToolName args" portion (not
          -- the leading timestamp/indent, which is now a real, if invisible,
          --  link so the row is clickable like every other row - padding
          -- would otherwise measure the link's raw HTML length instead of
          -- its rendered visual width and silently stop aligning anything).
          concat(
            '', formatDateTime(sr.timestamp, '%H:%i:%S'), '', '  ',
            if(sr.agent_invocation_id != '', '   ', ''),
            if(tr.escaped_args != '',
              -- Pad using tr.raw_args's PRE-ESCAPE length, not
              -- tr.escaped_args's - HTML-escaping expands '&'/'<'/'>' into
              -- multi-char entities ('>' etc.) that inflate the escaped
              -- string's character count without inflating its rendered
              -- width in the browser (a single '>' still renders as one
              -- '>' glyph). Padding/threshold math against the escaped
              -- string therefore under-padded any row whose args contained
              -- a shell redirect ('2>'), heredoc ('<<'), etc., pulling that
              -- row's duration/tokens/cost columns to the left relative to
              -- rows with no such characters - see this panel's own doc for
              -- the full diagnosis/repro. Only pad when the unpadded
              -- content already fits within the target width - beyond that,
              -- literal spaces are simply skipped (not truncated), which is
              -- what keeps the replaceOne() substring match below 100%
              -- reliable (rightPadUTF8 used to silently truncate mid-span
              -- when its input already exceeded the requested length,
              -- chopping straight through tr.escaped_args and leaving the
              -- argument text unwrapped - reproduced live with long
              -- file_path values, back when that field alone was allowed up
              -- to 120 chars against a 90-char pad width; every field cap is
              -- now unified to 90, and the pad width itself now comes from
              -- ${trace_width_budget} (117/120), see below). Padding itself is now literal spaces
              -- appended via repeat() to the already-built HTML (which
              -- still contains tr.escaped_args), rather than rightPadUTF8
              -- padding the HTML string directly - that was the other half
              -- of the same bug, since rightPadUTF8 was also being asked to
              -- measure width against escaped (entity-inflated) content.
              multiIf(
                lengthUTF8(concat('├─ ', if(lower(sr.tool_name) = 'web_search', 'WebSearch', sr.tool_name), ' ', tr.raw_args)) < if(sr.agent_invocation_id != '', (${trace_width_budget} - 3), ${trace_width_budget}),
                concat(
                  replaceOne(
                    concat('├─ ', if(lower(sr.tool_name) = 'web_search', 'WebSearch', sr.tool_name), ' ', tr.escaped_args),
                    tr.escaped_args,
                    concat('', tr.escaped_args, '')
                  ),
                  repeat(' ', greatest(0, if(sr.agent_invocation_id != '', (${trace_width_budget} - 3), ${trace_width_budget}) - lengthUTF8(concat('├─ ', if(lower(sr.tool_name) = 'web_search', 'WebSearch', sr.tool_name), ' ', tr.raw_args))))
                ),
                replaceOne(
                  concat('├─ ', if(lower(sr.tool_name) = 'web_search', 'WebSearch', sr.tool_name), ' ', tr.escaped_args),
                  tr.escaped_args,
                  concat('', tr.escaped_args, '')
                )
              ),
              rightPadUTF8(
                concat('├─ ', if(lower(sr.tool_name) = 'web_search', 'WebSearch', sr.tool_name)),
                if(sr.agent_invocation_id != '', (${trace_width_budget} - 3), ${trace_width_budget}), ' ')
            )
          ),
          concat(
            if(t2.litellm_call_id IS NOT NULL,
              concat('', formatDateTime(sr.timestamp, '%H:%i:%S'), ''),
              concat('', formatDateTime(sr.timestamp, '%H:%i:%S'), '')
            ), '  ',
            if(sr.agent_invocation_id != '', '   ', ''),
            '├─ ',
            -- Same total-width column budget as the regular tool-call branch
            -- above (${trace_width_budget} chars top-level, ${trace_width_budget} - 3
            -- nested - the 3-char indent eats INTO the shared total rather
            -- than being added on top of a flat content width, so nested
            -- rows' stats land in the same column as top-level rows' stats;
            -- see this panel's own doc for the "indent eats from the width
            -- budget" gotcha). trace_width_budget is a single hidden
            -- dashboard TextVariable (default 120) referenced unquoted here
            -- and in every other branch that shares this budget - see this
            -- panel's own doc, "Width budget as a dashboard variable"
            -- section, for why a Grafana variable (not a CTE constant) was
            -- used. This branch previously appended a flat '          ' (10
            -- spaces) regardless of content length, which is exactly why
            -- Agent-spawn rows (and same-branch tool-failure rows) never
            -- lined up with the formula-padded tool-call rows around them -
            -- a flat pad can't compensate for a description field that used
            -- to render fully unbounded. The description is pre-truncated
            -- (50 chars - deliberately NOT unified to the 90-char tool-arg
            -- cap, since this field isn't one of the tool_render argument
            -- branches the unification applies to) before padding, same
            -- "truncate first" safety rule used for tool-call args
            -- (tool_render's raw_args) so the pad-length computation is
            -- always a true upper bound on rendered width.
            if(sr.status = 'failure',
              concat('🚨 API error: ',
                replaceAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(
                  if(coalesce(fe.error_type, '') != '', fe.error_type, 'unknown'),
                  '&', '&'), '<', '<'), '>', '>'),
                ''
              ),
              concat(
                'Agent spawned: ',
                -- subagent_type now comes straight off this same row's own
                -- calculated_payload (populated by _classify_event's
                -- agent_spawn branch at ingest) instead of an ASOF join into
                -- agent_invocations on nearest spawned_at - the join is gone
                -- entirely, not just switched to litellm_call_id, since the
                -- data already lives on the row being rendered.
                replaceAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(
                  splitByChar('_', if(JSONExtractString(sr.calculated_payload, 'subagent_type') != '', JSONExtractString(sr.calculated_payload, 'subagent_type'), '?'))[1],
                  '&', '&'), '<', '<'), '>', '>'),
                '',
                -- description is now capped at 50 chars (was unbounded) so
                -- this row's rendered width is bounded and can actually be
                -- padded to the shared column budget below - see comment
                -- above.
                if(JSONExtractString(sr.calculated_payload, 'description') != '',
                  concat(' ',
                    replaceAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(
                      substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(sr.calculated_payload, 'description'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '), 1, 50),
                      '&', '&'), '<', '<'), '>', '>'),
                    ''
                  ), ''
                )
              )
            ),
            if(sr.status = 'failure', ' [FAIL]', ''),
            -- Pad using RAW (unescaped, pre-truncated) content length, not
            -- the escaped/HTML-tagged length above - same reasoning as
            -- tool_render's raw_args vs escaped_args: HTML entities and
            -- / tags inflate character count without inflating
            -- rendered width. Only pad when it already fits the target
            -- width; beyond that, spaces are simply skipped (never
            -- truncated after the fact), matching the tool-call branch.
            if(
              lengthUTF8(concat('├─ ',
                if(sr.status = 'failure',
                  concat('🚨 API error: ', if(coalesce(fe.error_type, '') != '', fe.error_type, 'unknown')),
                  concat('Agent spawned: ',
                    splitByChar('_', if(JSONExtractString(sr.calculated_payload, 'subagent_type') != '', JSONExtractString(sr.calculated_payload, 'subagent_type'), '?'))[1],
                    if(JSONExtractString(sr.calculated_payload, 'description') != '',
                      concat(' ', substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(sr.calculated_payload, 'description'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '), 1, 50)),
                      ''
                    )
                  )
                ),
                if(sr.status = 'failure', ' [FAIL]', '')
              )) < if(sr.agent_invocation_id != '', (${trace_width_budget} - 3), ${trace_width_budget}),
              repeat(' ', if(sr.agent_invocation_id != '', (${trace_width_budget} - 3), ${trace_width_budget}) - lengthUTF8(concat('├─ ',
                if(sr.status = 'failure',
                  concat('🚨 API error: ', if(coalesce(fe.error_type, '') != '', fe.error_type, 'unknown')),
                  concat('Agent spawned: ',
                    splitByChar('_', if(JSONExtractString(sr.calculated_payload, 'subagent_type') != '', JSONExtractString(sr.calculated_payload, 'subagent_type'), '?'))[1],
                    if(JSONExtractString(sr.calculated_payload, 'description') != '',
                      concat(' ', substringUTF8(replaceRegexpAll(JSONExtractString(sr.calculated_payload, 'description'), '[ \t\n\r]+', ' '), 1, 50)),
                      ''
                    )
                  )
                ),
                if(sr.status = 'failure', ' [FAIL]', '')
              ))),
              '  '
            )
          )
        )
      ),
      '', leftPad(if(sr.latency_ms IS NOT NULL, if(sr.latency_ms > 100, concat(toString(round(sr.latency_ms / 1000., 1)), 's'), concat(toString(sr.latency_ms), 'ms')), ''), 7, ' '), '',
      '', leftPad(if(u.tok > 0, if(u.tok >= 1000, concat(toString(round(u.tok / 1000., 1)), 'k'), toString(u.tok)), ''), 9, ' '), '',
      '', if(u.cost > 0, concat('   $', toString(round(u.cost, 4))), ''), ''
    ) AS line
  FROM scoped_events sr
  INNER JOIN first_real fr ON fr.session_id = sr.session_id
  LEFT JOIN usage_by_call u ON u.session_id = sr.session_id AND sr.litellm_call_id != '' AND u.litellm_call_id = sr.litellm_call_id
  LEFT JOIN tool_render tr ON tr.session_id = sr.session_id AND sr.litellm_call_id != '' AND tr.litellm_call_id = sr.litellm_call_id
  LEFT JOIN child_anchor ca ON ca.session_id = sr.session_id AND ca.agent_id = sr.agent_invocation_id
  LEFT JOIN failure_error fe ON fe.session_id = sr.session_id AND sr.litellm_call_id != '' AND fe.litellm_call_id = sr.litellm_call_id
  LEFT JOIN tie2_ts t2 ON t2.session_id = sr.session_id AND sr.litellm_call_id != '' AND t2.litellm_call_id = sr.litellm_call_id
  -- Suggestion-mode/title-gen/transcript-handoff rows are hidden entirely
  -- from tie=3 (their own reply text is harness-noise output, not a real
  -- answer to a real question) - is_sugg/is_title/is_transcript_handoff used
  -- to be recomputed here via the reply_flags CTE (a separate startsWith(
  -- prompt_text, ...) re-derivation, joined on the old x.timestamp =
  -- ev.timestamp pattern); both the join and the re-derivation are gone now
  -- that calculated_type already carries this same classification directly
  -- on sr itself (computed once, from this exact row's own prompt_text, at
  -- ingest).
  WHERE sr.timestamp >= fr.ts AND sr.calculated_type NOT IN ('suggestion_mode', 'title_gen', 'transcript_handoff')

  UNION ALL

  -- AskUserQuestion array elements rendered as nested grey lines - the sole
  -- display of this tool's args (tie=3's own tool-call row shows none, see
  -- tool_render above), one per questions[] entry read directly off this
  -- row's own calculated_payload (calculated_type = 'ask_user_question'),
  -- replacing the old join into tool_args_json (no longer needed - the
  -- questions array already lives on the same row being rendered).
  SELECT sr.session_id, sr.timestamp AS ts, if(sr.agent_invocation_id != '', coalesce(ca.anchor_ts, sr.timestamp), sr.timestamp) AS sort_ts, 4 AS tie,
    concat(
      '', formatDateTime(sr.timestamp, '%H:%i:%S'), '', '  ',
      if(sr.agent_invocation_id != '', '   ', ''),
      '○ ',  -- Use hollow circle for questions (read-only info)
      '',
      replaceRegexpAll(
        substringUTF8(replaceAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(question, '&', '&'), '<', '<'), '>', '>'), 1, 120),
        '\n', '
          '),
      if(lengthUTF8(question) > 120, '...', ''),
      ''
    ) AS line
  FROM scoped_events sr
  INNER JOIN first_real fr ON fr.session_id = sr.session_id
  LEFT JOIN child_anchor ca ON ca.session_id = sr.session_id AND ca.agent_id = sr.agent_invocation_id
  ARRAY JOIN arrayMap(x -> JSONExtractString(x, 'question'), JSONExtractArrayRaw(sr.calculated_payload, 'questions')) AS question
  WHERE sr.timestamp >= fr.ts
    AND sr.calculated_type = 'ask_user_question'
    AND sr.status != 'failure'
) AS display_rows
GROUP BY session_id
ORDER BY session_id

spec.title: Tool calls at $trace_ts
spec.description: Detail table for a single moment in the Trace panel above - click a timestamp link there (which carries that row's litellm_call_id) to list every tool call made by that exact agent_events row or, if the clicked row is an Agent spawn (▸ marker), all tool calls from the spawned child agent and one level of its own sub-agents (arbitrary nesting depth support (handles ingestion race where agent_invocation_id is blank on later rows)). Key = tool name, value = full arguments - use the magnifier/inspect icon on Arguments to see it in full. A single model call can invoke more than one tool in parallel, which the Trace tree above only shows the first of - this table shows all of them. Empty until a timestamp has been clicked ($trace_ts starts blank).

spec.rawSql:
WITH clicked_row AS (
  SELECT timestamp, tool_name, agent_invocation_id, session_id, litellm_call_id,
    calculated_type, calculated_payload, status, failed_tool_name, failed_tool_error
  FROM agent_events
  WHERE '$trace_ts' != ''
    AND litellm_call_id = '$trace_ts'
    AND (has([${session_id:singlequote}], agent_events.session_id))
),
agent_spawn_events AS (
  SELECT session_id, timestamp
  FROM agent_events
  WHERE calculated_type = 'agent_spawn' AND agent_invocation_id = ''
    AND agent_events.session_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT session_id FROM clicked_row)
),
clicked_event_info AS (
  SELECT
    cr.timestamp AS event_ts,
    cr.tool_name,
    cr.agent_invocation_id,
    cr.session_id,
    cr.status,
    cr.failed_tool_name,
    cr.failed_tool_error,
    if(cr.calculated_type = 'agent_spawn' AND cr.agent_invocation_id = '', 'spawn', 'non_spawn') AS click_type
  FROM clicked_row cr
),
-- For spawn clicks, find the next spawn's timestamp (if any) to bound the collection window
next_spawn_ts AS (
  SELECT
    cei.event_ts,
    argMinIf(ase.timestamp, ase.timestamp, ase.timestamp > cei.event_ts) AS next_spawn
  FROM clicked_event_info cei
  CROSS JOIN agent_spawn_events ase
  WHERE cei.click_type = 'spawn'
  GROUP BY cei.event_ts
),
-- Every calculated_type shape known to _classify_event is normalized here into a
-- single array of {"tool":...,"args":...} JSON objects, so both the direct and
-- descendant branches below can walk one uniform shape via a single
-- JSONExtractArrayRaw + arrayJoin, rather than re-deriving the OpenAI-style
-- tool_calls[].function.{name,arguments} shape from raw_payload as before.
-- 'tool_call' rows already carry the full parallel-call array under 'tools'
-- (built at ingest from every entry in that model turn's own tool_calls array -
-- see _classify_event) - skill_call/ask_user_question/agent_spawn rows only
-- describe themselves, so they're each wrapped into a synthetic one-element array
-- to keep the same downstream shape.
direct_tool_calls AS (
  SELECT JSONExtractString(t, 'tool') AS Tool,
         JSONExtractRaw(t, 'args') AS Arguments,
         ts AS Ts
  FROM (
    SELECT arrayJoin(
      multiIf(
        cr.calculated_type = 'tool_call', JSONExtractArrayRaw(cr.calculated_payload, 'tools'),
        cr.calculated_type = 'skill_call', [concat('{"tool":"Skill","args":', JSONExtractRaw(cr.calculated_payload, 'args'), '}')],
        cr.calculated_type = 'ask_user_question', [concat('{"tool":"AskUserQuestion","args":{"questions":', JSONExtractRaw(cr.calculated_payload, 'questions'), '}}')],
        cr.calculated_type = 'agent_spawn', [concat('{"tool":"Agent","args":', cr.calculated_payload, '}')],
        []
      )
    ) AS t, cr.timestamp AS ts
    FROM clicked_row cr
    CROSS JOIN clicked_event_info cei
    WHERE cei.click_type = 'non_spawn'
  ) x
),
-- Child and descendant tool calls: all events within spawn's execution window.
-- Timestamp-based matching (not strict agent_invocation_id equality) handles the
-- ingestion race where agent_invocation_id lands blank on some descendant rows,
-- and naturally covers arbitrary nesting depth (grandchildren, etc.) within the
-- same window.
child_and_descendant_tool_calls AS (
  SELECT JSONExtractString(t, 'tool') AS Tool,
         JSONExtractRaw(t, 'args') AS Arguments,
         ts AS Ts
  FROM (
    SELECT arrayJoin(
      multiIf(
        ae.calculated_type = 'tool_call', JSONExtractArrayRaw(ae.calculated_payload, 'tools'),
        ae.calculated_type = 'skill_call', [concat('{"tool":"Skill","args":', JSONExtractRaw(ae.calculated_payload, 'args'), '}')],
        ae.calculated_type = 'ask_user_question', [concat('{"tool":"AskUserQuestion","args":{"questions":', JSONExtractRaw(ae.calculated_payload, 'questions'), '}}')],
        ae.calculated_type = 'agent_spawn', [concat('{"tool":"Agent","args":', ae.calculated_payload, '}')],
        []
      )
    ) AS t,
    ae.timestamp AS ts
    FROM clicked_event_info cei
    LEFT JOIN next_spawn_ts nst ON nst.event_ts = cei.event_ts
    INNER JOIN agent_events ae ON ae.session_id = cei.session_id
    WHERE cei.click_type = 'spawn'
      AND ae.timestamp > cei.event_ts  -- After the spawn
      AND ae.timestamp != cei.event_ts  -- Not the spawn row itself
      -- Before next spawn (if exists; default DateTime64 is 1970-01-01)
      AND (nst.next_spawn = '1970-01-01T00:00:00'::DateTime64(6) OR ae.timestamp < nst.next_spawn)
  )
),
-- Error handling: surface failure rows separately. failed_tool_name/
-- failed_tool_error are their own columns (unaffected by raw_payload's removal),
-- so this branch is unchanged from before the schema migration.
error_rows AS (
  SELECT
    if(cei.failed_tool_name != '', cei.failed_tool_name, 'LLM_Error') AS Tool,
    cei.failed_tool_error AS Arguments,
    cei.event_ts AS Ts
  FROM clicked_event_info cei
  WHERE cei.status = 'failure' OR cei.failed_tool_name != ''
)
SELECT Tool, Arguments, Ts FROM direct_tool_calls
UNION ALL
SELECT Tool, Arguments, Ts FROM child_and_descendant_tool_calls
UNION ALL
SELECT Tool, Arguments, Ts FROM error_rows
ORDER BY Ts

spec.name: window
spec.label: Window
spec.description: Controls how timeseries data is grouped/aggregated over time (e.g. 1m = per minute); does not affect the selected time range.

spec.rawSql:
SELECT DISTINCT git_repo AS __value, git_repo AS __text FROM session_git_branch WHERE git_repo != ''
ORDER BY __value

SELECT DISTINCT git_branch AS __value, git_branch AS __text FROM session_git_branch WHERE git_branch != ''
ORDER BY __value

spec.name: provider
spec.label: Provider
spec.description: Filters the Model list below. Claude = claude-*, OpenAI = gpt-*/o-series/etc., Other = everything else.
spec.query: All : all, Claude : claude, OpenAI : openai, Other : other

spec.rawSql:
WITH filtered AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT model AS __value, model AS __text
  FROM agent_usage
  WHERE model != ''
    AND (
      '$provider' = 'all'
      OR ('$provider' = 'claude' AND match(model, '^claude-'))
      OR ('$provider' = 'openai' AND match(model, '^(gpt-|chatgpt-|o[0-9]|text-embedding-|dall-e-|whisper|tts-)'))
      OR ('$provider' = 'other' AND NOT match(model, '^(claude-|gpt-|chatgpt-|o[0-9]|text-embedding-|dall-e-|whisper|tts-)'))
    )
)
SELECT __value, __text FROM (
  SELECT __value, __text FROM filtered
  UNION ALL
  SELECT '__none__' AS __value, '(no models for this provider yet)' AS __text WHERE (SELECT count() FROM filtered) = 0
) ORDER BY __value

SELECT DISTINCT agent_name AS __value, agent_name AS __text FROM agent_events WHERE agent_name != ''
ORDER BY __value

SELECT DISTINCT skill_name AS __value, skill_name AS __text FROM agent_events WHERE skill_name != ''
ORDER BY __value

SELECT DISTINCT command_name AS __value, command_name AS __text FROM agent_events WHERE command_name != ''
ORDER BY __value

SELECT DISTINCT
  tool_name AS __value,
  replaceOne(tool_name, 'mcp__', '') AS __text
FROM agent_events
WHERE startsWith(tool_name, 'mcp__')
ORDER BY __value

spec.name: group_id
spec.label: Group
spec.description: LiteLLM Team a virtual key belongs to, keyed on the stable user_api_key_team_id (not the renamable team alias - see webhook/src/clickhouse_ingest.py:_group_id/_group_alias) - empty for every user until LiteLLM Teams are configured (README "LiteLLM" – "Once it's needed: Teams..."), which is an operator action outside this dashboard.

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  g.group_id AS __value,
  coalesce(any(grp.group_name), g.group_id) AS __text
FROM agent_events g
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT group_id, argMax(group_name, updated_at) AS group_name
  FROM ai_gateway_groups
  GROUP BY group_id
) grp ON grp.group_id = g.group_id
WHERE g.group_id != ''
GROUP BY g.group_id
ORDER BY __text

SELECT
  e.user_id AS __value,
  coalesce(any(usr.user_name), e.user_id) AS __text
FROM agent_events e
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT user_id, argMax(user_name, updated_at) AS user_name
  FROM ai_gateway_users
  GROUP BY user_id
) usr ON usr.user_id = e.user_id
WHERE e.user_id NOT IN ('', 'unknown-user')
  AND (has([${group_id:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${group_id:singlequote}], e.group_id))
GROUP BY e.user_id
ORDER BY __value

spec.name: session_id
spec.label: Session

spec.rawSql:
SELECT __value, __text FROM (
  SELECT '' AS __value, 'None' AS __text, toDateTime64('2100-01-01', 3) AS __ts
  UNION ALL
  SELECT
    e.session_id AS __value,
    concat(coalesce(any(usr.user_name), any(e.user_id)), ' - ', e.session_id) AS __text,
    min(e.timestamp) AS __ts
  FROM agent_events e
  LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT user_id, argMax(user_name, updated_at) AS user_name
    FROM ai_gateway_users
    GROUP BY user_id
  ) usr ON usr.user_id = e.user_id
  WHERE e.session_id != ''
    AND (has([${user_id:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${user_id:singlequote}], e.user_id))
    AND (has([${group_id:singlequote}], '__all__') OR has([${group_id:singlequote}], e.group_id))
  GROUP BY e.session_id
  ORDER BY __ts DESC
  LIMIT 200
)
ORDER BY __ts DESC

spec.description: Value format: <user_id> - <session_id>

spec.name: trace_ts
spec.label: Trace timestamp
spec.description: Hidden - set by clicking a timestamp link in the Trace panel; the trace-detail table below it reads this to show every tool call made at that exact moment.

spec.name: trace_width_budget
spec.label: Trace width budget
spec.description: Hidden - single source of truth for the Trace panel's (panel-76) total-line character-width budget used to align tool-call/agent-spawn stats columns (duration/tokens/cost). Referenced unquoted (bare number, no :singlequote) as ${trace_width_budget} inside panel-76's rawSql, including in arithmetic like (${trace_width_budget} - 3) for the nested-row indent deduction. Edit this variable's value to retune the width instead of hunting for scattered literals in rawSql.

spec.name: include_cache_tokens
spec.label: Include cache tokens
spec.description: Controls whether every 'tokens'/'input tokens' panel on this dashboard includes prompt-cache tokens. LiteLLM folds cache_creation_tokens and cache_read_tokens into input_tokens by default for cache-capable providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, and others), so headline token totals are cache-inclusive unless this is switched off. On (default) = today's numbers, unchanged. Off = input_tokens minus cache_creation_tokens and cache_read_tokens, i.e. net/fresh input tokens only. Output tokens are never affected by this toggle.
